dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the captain knows we've seen the ledgers. Things are moving fast.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two guards came asking pointed questions about where I've been. {target_npc} was warned to stay quiet. The captain is moving the evidence: crates loaded onto a cart heading {location_hint} under heavy escort. If that cart reaches its destination, {quest_focus} disappears forever. Watch out for {enemy_type} along the road: {quest_threat} is using them as a screen.
dialogueChunks.outro: The captain is panicking. Panicked people are dangerous, but they also make mistakes. Don't let the evidence vanish.

playerResponses.text: A cart full of evidence heading out of town. I'll follow it.
Panicked and dangerous. My favorite combination. I'm on it.
Following an armed escort into the wilderness? That's how people disappear.

dialogueChunks.intro: Someone broke into my home last night, {player_name}. They took the proof I'd been keeping.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Everything I'd documented about {quest_focus}: stolen. The healer must have realized I know. {target_npc} says they've been acting erratically, canceling appointments. They're planning to run. I need you to find where they hid the stolen evidence: {target_npc} saw them heading toward an old storage building {location_hint}. And I need {quest_item} urgently: {quest_stakes}, and patients need real treatment now.
dialogueChunks.outro: They stole back the proof, but they can't steal what's in my memory. If we can recover even one piece, the council will listen.

playerResponses.text: The evidence is out there. I'll track it down and bring proper medicine too.
Breaking into your home to destroy proof. Desperation makes people bold.
If they're willing to break into homes, they're willing to do worse. Too dangerous.

dialogueChunks.intro: They did it, {player_name}. {target_npc} went to the council and accused me of the theft.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: False charges, fabricated ledgers: {quest_threat} was prepared in advance. I have three days before the council rules. If I can't produce evidence of {quest_focus}, I'm the one who gets exiled. I need you to find the cache {location_hint}: the actual stolen goods with {target_npc}'s markings. The {enemy_type} will make it difficult, and I suspect traps have been set.
dialogueChunks.outro: Three days to prove I'm not the thief. Three days to prove {quest_origin}. Everything rides on what you find.

playerResponses.text: Three days. I won't waste a minute. Where's the cache?
Framing the accuser. Classic move. I'll find the real evidence.
If the council already has their mind made up, evidence won't help.
